originalTitle: Weight-loss treatments boom as Kenyan attitudes to beauty change
title: Super Stars, Super Looks! What's New in Kenya?
content:
Hello everyone! It's your friendly reporter, coming to you live from beautiful Africa, from a country called Kenya! Today, we have some exciting news to talk about. It’s about famous people, just like the stars you see on TV or hear on the radio! These famous people, called "celebrities," are doing something new. They are changing how they look!

What's Happening in Kenya?

In Kenya, many people are talking about how they look. They want to feel good and look good too. Some people want to become a little thinner. This means they want their bodies to be smaller. They are trying special ways to do this. These ways are like treatments to help them lose some weight. Sometimes, people also want to change other parts of their body. Maybe they want a different nose or smoother skin. These are called "cosmetic procedures." It's like giving your body a little makeover! Did you know? Kenya is a very special place! It has amazing animals like lions, elephants, and giraffes! It also has tall mountains and beautiful beaches.

Who is Doing This? Famous Stars!

Guess who is doing these things a lot? It's the famous people we just talked about! These are the people you admire. They sing, they act, they are on TV shows. When famous people do something new, other people often want to try it too. It’s like when your friend gets a new toy, and you want to play with it! So, many people in Kenya are now thinking about changing how they look. They see their favourite stars and want to look like them. A "celebrity" is just a famous person that many people know and like. They are often very good at something, like singing or acting. Did you know? Many famous runners from Kenya are super fast and win big races all over the world! They show us how strong and healthy Kenyan people can be.

Why Do People Want to Change?

Sometimes, people think that if they look a certain way, they will be more beautiful or more liked. They might see lots of pictures on their parents' phones or on TV. These pictures often show people who are very thin or have perfect skin. This can make some people feel like they need to change to be beautiful too. But guess what? "Beautiful" means many different things! A flower is beautiful, a rainbow is beautiful, and you are beautiful just the way you are! It is important to remember that everyone is special and unique. We all look different, and that makes the world colourful and fun! Did you know? The Maasai people in Kenya wear very colourful clothes and beads. They think it makes them look very strong and beautiful! Everyone has their own idea of what beauty is.

Are There Worries? Yes, Some Grown-ups Are Talking.

Now, some grown-ups are a little bit worried about all this. They have some important things to say. They worry that some of these treatments might not be super safe. It's like playing a game – you want to make sure you don't get hurt! They also worry that people might forget what is most important: being healthy and happy! Being healthy means eating good food, playing outside, and getting enough sleep. When you are healthy, your body is strong! It's good to want to look nice, but it's even better to be strong inside and out. Did you know? Eating yummy fruits like mangoes and bananas can make you strong and healthy! Kenya grows many delicious fruits.

What Should We Remember?

So, what is the big lesson for us? It's super important to be happy with how *you* look. Your body is amazing! It helps you run, jump, sing, and hug your family. Being kind to others, being smart in school, and being healthy are super, super important things. These things make you shine! Grown-ups should help all kids feel good about themselves. They should teach us that everyone is special. Let's celebrate all kinds of beauty! Big, small, tall, short, light skin, dark skin – everyone is wonderful! Did you know? Your smile is one of the most beautiful things about you! When you smile, you make others happy too. So, in Kenya, famous people are choosing to change how they look. But remember, the most important thing for all of us is to be healthy, happy, and to love ourselves just the way we are. You are simply amazing!
summary: Famous people in Kenya are changing how they look to be thinner or different, but grown-ups worry about being healthy and happy with ourselves, which is more important.
whyMatters: It matters because we should all know that being healthy and happy with our bodies is more important than trying to look like someone else, especially famous people on TV.

originalTitle: Heavy rains and flooding kills at least 23 in Nairobi
title: Super Big Rains in Nairobi!
content:
Hello, amazing kids! It's your friendly news reporter, Kojo, here from Africa! I'm here to tell you about some important news from a big, busy city called Nairobi. Nairobi is in a country called Kenya. It's a beautiful place with lots of tall buildings and friendly people.

Super Big Rains in Nairobi!

Recently, something very big happened in Nairobi. The sky opened up, and it rained, rained, and rained! It wasn't just a little sprinkle; it was a super-duper, heavy, non-stop rain for many, many hours. Imagine a giant shower coming down from the sky, non-stop! All this rain made a lot of water come down very, very fast. The ground couldn't drink up all the water quickly enough. When there's too much water and nowhere for it to go, it starts to cover everything. This is what we call a flood.

When Water Goes Everywhere: Floods!

So, in Nairobi, the streets and even some homes started to get covered with water. It was like a giant puddle everywhere you looked! Some places had water that was very, very deep. When the water gets so deep, it makes everything very tricky and sometimes dangerous. Cars can't drive through it, and sometimes people can't even walk safely. It's important to know that flood water can be very dangerous and even carry things that can make us sick. Did you know? Water is super amazing! It helps plants grow, and we need it to drink every day to stay healthy. But too much water in the wrong place can cause big problems like floods.

Making Things Tricky for Planes and Cars

The big rains didn't just affect the streets. They also made things difficult at the Nairobi Airport. An airport is where big planes fly in and out. These planes take people to different cities and countries, carrying special cargo too. Because of the very heavy rain and all the water, it was hard for the planes to take off or land safely. So, many planes had to wait, or their journeys were changed. Imagine wanting to go on a fun trip, but your plane has to wait because of a giant puddle on the runway! On the roads, it was even trickier. Many roads in Nairobi, especially in the capital city part, became like rivers. Cars and buses couldn't drive through the deep water safely. People had to find other ways to get around, or just stay home to be safe. It made it hard for people to go to work or for kids to go to school.

Keeping Everyone Safe

Sadly, because the water was so deep and fast in some places, it caused a lot of trouble. Some houses got damaged, and sadly, some people got very hurt. And very sadly, about 23 people even lost their lives because of these big floods. It's a very sad time for many families in Nairobi. But do you know what else happened? Many kind people came out to help! Rescue workers, like brave firefighters and police, worked very hard. They used boats and special equipment to help people who were stuck in their homes or cars. They are true heroes! Neighbours also helped neighbours. They shared food and safe places to stay. It shows that even when things are tough, people in Africa, and all over the world, care for each other and help each other. Did you know? Rescue workers are like real-life superheroes! They train very hard to help people when there's danger, like during a flood or a big fire.

What We Can Do When It Rains a Lot

So, my young friends, what can YOU do if there's very heavy rain and flooding where you live? The most important thing is to stay safe! 1. **Stay Inside!** If your grown-ups say to stay home, please do. Your home, if it's safe, is usually the safest place to be during a big storm. 2. **Don't Play in Flood Water!** Flood water might look like a giant puddle, but it's not safe to play in. It can have hidden dangers like sharp objects, electricity poles that might fall, or even invisible germs that can make you sick. It can also be deeper and move faster than it looks. 3. **Listen to Grown-ups!** Always listen to your parents, teachers, or any other grown-ups who are looking after you. They know what's best to keep you safe and sound. 4. **Help Others (Safely)!** If you can, help your grown-ups by carrying light things, or by keeping younger brothers and sisters calm and safe inside. But always make sure you are safe first! Did you know? Flood water can sometimes have invisible germs in it from drains and pipes. That's why we should never, ever play in it!

Nairobi is Strong!

Even though the floods caused a lot of problems and sadness, the people of Nairobi are very strong. They are already working hard to clean up their city. The water is starting to go down in many places, and people are helping to fix things and make their homes safe again. The city will get back to normal, and people will keep helping each other. Just like after any big storm, the sun will shine again and help dry everything. And even though too much rain caused problems this time, remember that rain is also very important! It helps our plants and trees grow green and strong. It fills up our rivers and lakes so we have water to drink. Did you know? Nairobi is also called the "Green City in the Sun" because it has so many beautiful parks and trees! It's a lovely place with lots of nature.

Let's Stay Safe and Help!

So, my young friends, that's the news from Nairobi. Remember to always be safe, listen to your grown-ups, and if you see someone who needs help, tell a grown-up right away. We are all one big family in Africa, and we look out for each other, especially when things are tough. Let's send our love and strength to everyone in Nairobi. And let's all do our part to keep our communities safe and clean. Until next time, stay curious and stay safe! This is Kojo, your friendly news reporter, signing off!
summary: Heavy rains in Nairobi, Kenya, caused big floods, making roads and the airport tricky and sadly hurting people, but many kind helpers are working to keep everyone safe and clean up.
whyMatters: This is important because floods can make people's homes unsafe, stop planes and cars, and make people very sad, so we need to know how to stay safe and help each other when it rains a lot.

title: 'Massive' numbers killed by gunmen in latest Nigeria attack, senator tells BBC
content: Suspected Islamist militants reportedly seized control of a village for two days before being forced out by troops and air strikes.

originalTitle: Iran will not ‘bow’ to US and Israeli pressure, Pezeshkian says
title: President Pezeshkian Says Iran Will Stand Tall!
content:
Hello, amazing friends all across Africa! It's your friendly news reporter here. I'm so happy to share some exciting news with you today. We're going on a little adventure in our minds, all the way to a country far, far away called Iran. It's a beautiful land with big deserts, tall mountains, and lots of wonderful people, just like you! We heard some special news about the leader of Iran. His name is President Masoud Pezeshkian. He said something very important. He said that Iran will not 'bow' to some requests from two other countries. These countries are the United States and Israel. What does that mean?

What is a President?

First, let's talk about what a President is. A President is like the main leader of a whole country! Think of your school principal who helps make big rules for your school, or the head of your family who helps everyone make good choices. A President does that for an entire country! President Pezeshkian's job is to make big decisions for Iran. He wants to keep all the people in Iran safe, happy, and help their country grow strong. It's a very important job, filled with lots of responsibility. Did you know? Many of the wonderful countries right here in Africa also have Presidents! They all work hard to lead their people and their lands.

Countries are Like Big Families!

Now, let's think about countries. A country is like a very, very big family! It's a special place where many people live together. They often share a common language, culture, and history. Iran is one country, the United States is another, and Israel is another. Just like in your family or with your friends, sometimes different families or different friends have different ideas about things. They might not always agree on the best way to do something. That's okay! It happens all the time, even with big countries. Did you know? Africa is a huge continent with 54 different countries! That's a lot of big families, all with their own unique ways and leaders!

What Does "Pressure" Mean?

President Pezeshkian said Iran will not 'bow' to 'pressure'. What does 'pressure' mean? Let's imagine your friend really wants you to play their game, but you really want to play your game. Your friend might keep asking you, or try to convince you over and over. That's a kind of 'pressure'. They are trying to make you do what they want. In the news, when countries talk about 'pressure', it means other countries might be asking them to do things in a certain way. They might be trying to convince Iran to change some of its plans or rules. But President Pezeshkian said, "No, thank you! We will decide what is best for us and our people." Did you know? Talking things out calmly is almost always the best way for people and countries to solve problems, even if they have different ideas!

Standing Strong Like a Baobab Tree!

When President Pezeshkian says Iran will not 'bow', it doesn't mean he's literally bending over! It means he wants Iran to stand strong and firm, like a mighty Baobab tree that doesn't bend even in a strong wind. Think about the amazing Baobab trees we see in many parts of Africa. They are so big and strong, standing tall for hundreds, even thousands of years! They are rooted deeply and stay firm. That's how President Pezeshkian wants his country to be – strong, independent, and making its own choices for its people. He wants to protect Iran's way of life and ensure it can grow and thrive. He is standing up for what he believes is right for his country. It's like you standing up for yourself in a kind and firm way when someone asks you to do something you don't think is right. Did you know? Baobab trees are sometimes called the 'Tree of Life' because they provide food, water, and shelter for many animals and people in Africa!

Talking is Important!

Even when countries have different ideas or leaders make strong statements, it's very important for them to keep talking to each other. Countries talk about many things, like how to share resources, how to help each other when there are problems, or how to keep the air and water clean for everyone. Sometimes they agree, and sometimes they don't. But talking helps them understand each other better. It's like when you and your friends talk about what game to play. You might not agree at first, but by talking, you can find a game everyone likes. Did you know? There is a very big group called the United Nations where almost all the countries in the world come together to talk. They try their best to solve big problems and keep the world peaceful!

Wishing for Peace and Friendship

So, President Pezeshkian made a strong statement for his country, Iran. He showed that Iran wants to be independent and make its own decisions. Even when leaders of different countries have different ideas, everyone still hopes for peace and understanding. Children all around the world, including you here in Africa and children in Iran, the United States, and Israel, all want the same things: to play, to learn, to be safe, and to have happy families. We all share this one amazing planet Earth. It's important to be kind and try to understand each other, even when we are different. Because friendship and peace are the best gifts we can give each other. Thank you for listening, my wonderful friends! Until next time, stay curious and stay kind! Did you know? When children from different countries become friends, they help build bridges between their families and their countries, making the world a friendlier place for everyone!
summary: Iran's leader, President Pezeshkian, said his country will bravely make its own choices and not listen to requests from the United States or Israel, standing strong for what Iran believes is best.
whyMatters: It matters because it shows how leaders of countries make big choices to keep their people and their land safe and strong, even when other countries have different ideas about what should be done.
